title: Bellucia huberi
intro: Bellucia huberi, also known as the "Red-bellied Bellucia" or "Quebra Pedra," is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as potential uses in treating conditions such as fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Bellucia huberi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Bellucia huberi is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Bellucia huberi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. This family is commonly known as the "melastome" or "memecyclid" family. The Melastomataceae family is part of the larger order Myrtales, which also includes other families such as the Myrtaceae and the Penaeaceae. The Melastomataceae family is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world, and it consists of over 170 genera and 5,000 species.
